 CLAUDE.md Current State.

 Session 51 shipped all Tier B + C Lead Generation features (F5–F10),

 fixed the F4+F2 source URL bug, and completely rewrote F2 around

 BoardDocs + RFP queries. Nothing has been acted on yet — Steven

 explicitly deferred all acting. The next-session checklist groups the

 action items at the top.

 Start by telling me what's in the checklist and asking which items I

 want to tackle first. Don't just start running scanners — ask me

 first, because several of them write to the Signals tab and may

 dedup-collide with Session 49's stale data (and because we may need

 to act on F1 prospects, Tulsa bond results, or the 4 strong enriched

 signals before running more scans).

 Also check for the following cached state from Session 51:

 - /tmp/scout_venv exists with project requirements installed — I

 used it to run local smoke tests without polluting system Python.

 Safe to reuse if still present.

 - The old F2 Session 49 signals (7 noise entries) are still in the

 Signals tab with empty URL columns. They need to be cleared or

 they'll dedup-block the new BoardDocs-sourced signals when

 /signal_competitors re-runs.

 Ready to code?
 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 Session 52 Plan — Audit + Act on Session 51 Builds

 Context

 Session 51 shipped F2 (rewrite), F5, F6, F7, F8, F9, F10 — all Tier B + C Lead Gen features — without entering plan mode or discussing approach. Steven flagged this and asked for (a) a careful audit of what

 got built and (b) a staged plan to act on the outputs, fix issues, and work the existing backlog (Tulsa bond, 4 STRONG signals, 384 F1 prospects).

 The session is audit-first, then fix blockers, then act . Nothing gets queued in bulk until the priority-scoring bug is patched.

 A feedback memory feedback_always_plan_mode.md has been added to prevent autonomous builds going forward.

 ---

 Code Review Summary — Session 51 Builds

 Three Explore agents reviewed F2/F5, F6/F7/F8/F10, and F9. Confirmed the two biggest findings against live code.

 🔴 BLOCKER 1 — Priority scoring collapses to base for all new strategies

 File: tools/district_prospector.py:2515

 What's wrong: add_district() calls _calculate_priority(strategy, 0, 0, 0) with positional zeros and no **kwargs . But the new strategy branches rely on kwargs:

 - charter_cmo → kwargs.get("school_count", 0) → always 0 → every CMO scores 780 flat

 - cte_center → kwargs.get("sending_districts", 0) → always 0 → every center scores 760 flat

 - private_school_network → kwargs.get("schools", 0) → always 0 → every network scores 740 flat

 - compliance_gap / csta_partnership / competitor_displacement / cs_funding_recipient / homeschool_coop → all use est_enrollment which is also passed as 0 → they all collapse to their tier base

 Impact: F6 queues IDEA PS (135 schools) at the same priority as a 3-school CMO. F7 queues a 50-district regional center at the same priority as a 2-district one. Size-leverage signal is lost across every new

 strategy. If we queue-and-approve in bulk tonight, sort order is meaningless.

 Fix: Add **kwargs to add_district() signature, forward to _calculate_priority() , and update charter_prospector , cte_prospector , private_schools , and compliance_gap_scanner to pass the size kwargs they already

 have. Also write enrollment / school_count / sending_districts into the Prospecting Queue row columns (they're currently dropped into Notes as unstructured text).

 ---

 🔴 BLOCKER 2 — F9 priority tier contradicts its own docstring

 File: tools/district_prospector.py:585-592 , tools/compliance_gap_scanner.py docstring

 What's wrong: Docstring says "just below cs_funding_recipient". Numerically, compliance_gap is 850-939 and cs_funding_recipient is 800-899 — compliance_gap is ABOVE funding, making it the highest-priority

 non-upward strategy in the entire system . For an unvalidated pilot that requires 60% manual validation before scaling, this puts unvetted prospects at the top of the work queue, displacing warm leads we

 already trust.

 Fix: Pick one — either (a) retier to 760-799 (below funding, above cold, acknowledges legal urgency without overweighting unvalidated data) and update docstring, or (b) accept "law is the sales pitch" framing

 and rewrite the docstring to say "above funding". My recommendation: (a) for the pilot. Revisit after 60% validation rate proven.

 ---

 🟡 SHOULD-FIX — F9 has no cost guardrail or validation tracking

 Files: tools/compliance_gap_scanner.py , agent/main.py:3408-3431

 - /scan_compliance dispatches directly with no confirmation. Typo /scan_compliance ALL or a double-tap could burn $4-$16.

 - 60% validation exit criterion is handwave-only — no log file, no /mark_validated command, no cumulative tracking. We could scale to 5 more states thinking we hit the bar when we actually validated 25%.

 - Claude response parsing is silent on malformed JSON — returns [] with no distinction between "no districts found" and "extraction failed".

 Fix:

 1. Add per-state-per-day rate limit in scanner entry (skip if scanned in last 24h).

 2. Create memory/compliance_gap_validation_log.json + /validate_compliance [prospect_name] [yes|no] [notes] command.

 3. Add explicit schema validation on Claude response, log parse failures distinctly.

 ---

 🟡 SHOULD-FIX — F5 CSTA scanner missing kill switch

 File: tools/signal_processor.py:4565

 F2 has ENABLE_COMPETITOR_SCAN , F4 has ENABLE_FUNDING_SCAN , F9 has ENABLE_COMPLIANCE_SCAN . F5 CSTA has none. Per project rule feedback_kill_switches , new scanners must ship with a one-line disable. Same issue

 for F8 private_schools.py and F10 discover_homeschool_coops in signal_processor.

 Fix: Add ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN , ENABLE_PRIVATE_SCHOOL_DISCOVERY , ENABLE_HOMESCHOOL_COOP_DISCOVERY constants near the top of their modules; guard function entry points.

 ---

 🟡 SHOULD-FIX — F2 evidence-type case sensitivity + prompt/code mismatch

 File: tools/signal_processor.py:3565, 3674-3675

 - Prompt enum lists rfp_replacement , but _STRONG_EVIDENCE tuple checks for both rfp_replacement AND rfp_bid . rfp_bid is never in the schema — dead code or latent hallucination guard?

 - Evidence_type is not .lower() ed before comparison. If Claude returns Case_Study or BOARD_ADOPTION , the HIGH gate silently fails.

 - Same bug exists in F5 CSTA at line 4721 : evidence_type = (item.get("evidence_type") or "active_member").strip() — no .lower() .

 Fix: Normalize to lowercase after strip in both scanners. Resolve rfp_bid vs rfp_replacement — pick one, update the other.

 ---

 🟡 SHOULD-FIX — F8 diocese-as-district is a research-engine anti-pattern

 File: tools/private_schools.py:207-245

 The scanner queues "Archdiocese of Los Angeles Schools" as a district. But dioceses have no NCES ID, no CCD enrollment, no board meetings, no single superintendent email. When research_engine runs on a diocese

 entity, Phase B (domain discovery), Phase C (contact extraction), and Phase E (verification) will all partially fail or return school-level data the queue can't represent.

 Fix: Two options:

 - (a) Short-term: Flag diocese networks with strategy="private_school_network" but route them past research_engine — skip the standard pipeline, use a simpler "look up contacts on website" flow, and accept

 lower enrichment quality.

 - (b) Long-term: Pivot to school-by-school queueing within a parent network. Queue the top 5-10 schools per diocese instead of the diocese itself.

 My recommendation: (a) for this session. Don't queue the 24 networks yet — we need a separate research playbook first.

 ---

 🟡 SHOULD-FIX — F2 dedup will duplicate on rescan, not block

 File: tools/signal_processor.py:752

 Dedup key is f"{msg_id}|{url}" if url else msg_id . Session 49 F2 signals have empty URL columns. When we re-scan BoardDocs and extract the same district+competitor, new rows have a real URL → different dedup

 key → duplicate rows in the Signals tab , not dedup-block.

 So the concern in the session handoff ("old rows will dedup-block the new BoardDocs signals") is actually backwards: they'll co-exist as orphaned legacy + fresh URL-bearing rows. Still ugly.

 Fix: Manually clear the 7 Session 49 F2 competitor rows from the Signals tab before re-running /signal_competitors . No code change needed.

 ---

 🟢 NITs (defer to Session 53)

 - F2: name_to_key fallback silently keys on Claude typos ( Tinker → new key). Log a warning.

 - F2: NCES lookup skip-reasons not surfaced in return dict.

 - F5: CSTA prompt doesn't document that conference_speaker / active_member won't auto-queue (only chapter_officer / board_member will).

 - F9: PDF 10 MB cap enforced after chunk append (could overrun by one chunk).

 - F9: Anthropic client timeout is 180s; project standard is 90s.

 - F8: Homeschool co-op size heuristic leaves most results as unknown .

 - F6/F7: charter_prospector and cte_prospector are near-identical — candidate for consolidation to generic seed_prospector.py (not a bug, just tech debt).

 ---

 Stage 1 — Housekeeping (read-only, no API cost)

 1. Clear 7 Session 49 F2 competitor signals from Signals tab (via Google Sheet UI or a one-off script).

 2. Confirm /tmp/scout_venv still has deps installed — quick sanity import test.

 Stage 2 — Time-sensitive backlog (act before the code fixes)

 3. Tulsa PS bond check. Vote was April 7, two days ago. Web search for "Tulsa Public Schools bond Prop 3 April 7 2026 results". If Prop 3 ($104M tech) passed → surface Robert F. Burton (Exec Dir IT) for

 outreach. Don't use the scanner — this is a single manual lookup.

 4. Verify Google Alert parser. Run /signal_scan to confirm the first post-April-9 digest parses correctly with new bond/leadership/AI-policy sections.

 5. Act on 4 STRONG enriched signals from Session 44 if still fresh: Tulsa PS, Richardson ISD, Acton-Boxborough, Norwalk PS. Draft outreach for each (Steven-voice, via existing email drafter patterns). No new

 code needed.

 Stage 3 — Fix BLOCKERs (code changes)

 6. Fix priority collapse (BLOCKER 1):

 - tools/district_prospector.py : add **kwargs to add_district() , forward to _calculate_priority() , add new columns ( est_enrollment , school_count , sending_districts , schools ) to the Prospecting Queue row

 write.

 - tools/charter_prospector.py : pass school_count=cmo["school_count"] and est_enrollment=cmo["est_enrollment"] to add_district() .

 - tools/cte_prospector.py : pass sending_districts=center["sending_districts"] and est_enrollment=center["est_enrollment"] .

 - tools/private_schools.py : pass schools=network["schools"] .

 - tools/compliance_gap_scanner.py : pass est_enrollment when available (may be absent — OK to pass 0).

 - tools/signal_processor.py : in scan_competitors and scan_csta_chapters , pass est_enrollment from NCES lookup when queueing.

 - Local smoke test: queue 2 test CMOs (big + small) and confirm priorities differ.

 7. Fix F9 priority tier (BLOCKER 2):

 - tools/district_prospector.py:585-592 : change compliance_gap tier to 760 + min(int(est_enrollment / 200), 99) = 760-859 (above cold small, below funding). Update docstring to match.

 - Update tools/CLAUDE.md compliance_gap block.

 Stage 4 — Fix SHOULD-FIX items (lean pass, not exhaustive)

 8. Add kill switches: ENABLE_CSTA_SCAN , ENABLE_PRIVATE_SCHOOL_DISCOVERY , ENABLE_HOMESCHOOL_COOP_DISCOVERY .

 9. F2+F5 evidence-type case normalization: add .lower() at the strip points. Resolve rfp_bid vs rfp_replacement (keep rfp_replacement , delete rfp_bid from the tuple).

 10. F9 cost guardrail: add per-state-per-day rate limit inside scan_compliance_gaps() .

 11. F9 validation tracking: create memory/compliance_gap_validation_log.json + /validate_compliance direct-dispatch handler.

 12. F9 schema validation: log parse failures distinctly from "no districts found".

 13. F8 diocese routing: mark private_school_network as not-yet-queueable . Disable /prospect_private_networks with a clear message: "Diocese research playbook deferred — see Session 52 plan." Revisit after a

 research playbook exists.

 14. Commit each fix separately per feedback_per_feature_commits rule.

 Stage 5 — Rebuild Signals with corrected scanners

 15. /signal_funding — rebuild F4 Signals with URLs captured (post-fix commit).

 16. /signal_competitors — rebuild F2 Signals with new BoardDocs-sourced HIGH signals. Manual spot-check 3-5 top HIGH signals before trusting auto-queue.

 17. /signal_csta — rebuild with URLs + kill switch + case normalization. Manual spot-check auto-queued chapter leaders.

 Stage 6 — Queue seed prospectors (after priority fix)

 18. /prospect_charter_cmos — queue all 43 CMOs with corrected priorities. Review top 10 by priority before batch-approving.

 19. /prospect_cte_centers — queue 79 centers with corrected priorities. Review top 10.

 20. Skip F8 /prospect_private_networks for this session — blocked on diocese research playbook.

 21. Approve batches of 5-10 via /prospect_approve .

 Stage 7 — F9 compliance pilot (after fixes + guardrails)

 22. /scan_compliance CA — smallest, most mature law, best place to start. Cost ~$0.50-$2.00.

 23. Manually validate first 5-10 extracted districts via Serper + evidence_quote review. Log each in compliance_gap_validation_log.json via /validate_compliance .

 24. Only if first-pass validation rate ≥60% → run /scan_compliance IL and /scan_compliance MA .

 25. If validation rate <60% → revise prompt, re-run CA, don't scale.

 Stage 8 — F1 backlog

 26. Approve 384 F1 intra-district prospects in batches of 5-10 via /prospect_approve . Takes multiple sessions; start the first 30-50 this session.

 ---

 Critical Files to Modify (Stage 3-4 only)

 Verification

 - Priority fix smoke test: After Stage 3 step 6, run a local Python snippet against /tmp/scout_venv that calls _calculate_priority("charter_cmo", 0, 0, 0, school_count=135) vs school_count=3 and confirm 135 >

 3 in priority. Repeat for cte_center , compliance_gap , etc.

 - Compliance tier fix: Confirm _calculate_priority("compliance_gap", 0, 0, 50000) < _calculate_priority("cs_funding_recipient", 0, 0, 50000) .

 - Scanner smoke tests: Run /signal_competitors and /signal_csta and confirm the auto-queued rows have non-empty Est. Enrollment and Source URL columns.

 - F9 rate limit: Run /scan_compliance CA twice in a row; second call should return "already scanned today".

 - Validation log: Run /validate_compliance "Test District" yes "manual check confirmed" ; inspect the JSON log.

 - Full smoke: After all fixes + commits, run /signals to confirm no regression in signal rendering, then /prospect_all to spot-check the new seeded prospects show size metadata.

 ---

 Out of Scope for Session 52

 - Consolidating charter_prospector + cte_prospector into seed_prospector.py (tech debt, not blocking)

 - F8 diocese research playbook redesign (separate planning session)

 - Firecrawl paid plan (budget)

 - F2 competitor scanner NITs (logging improvements)

 - Approving all 384 F1 prospects in one session (too many, spread across sessions)

 - Scaling F9 beyond CA/IL/MA (gated on 60% validation)

 T ell Claude what to change

 .

 Refine this plan. Your job is to pressure-test it
 honestly — keep what's genuinely strong, rebuild what isn't, and hand back
 something I can execute with full confidence.
 Act as a ruthless, senior-level technical reviewer and architect. Be direct,

 specific, and opinionated. No hedging, no diplomacy, no soft language around

 real problems. If something is wrong, say it's wrong and say why. But the

 inverse also holds: if something is right, leave it alone. Do not rewrite solid

 work just to look thorough — churn for its own sake is its own kind of

 failure. The bar for changing something is that the new version is meaningfully

 better, not merely different.

 happen because the reviewer started reacting before they finished

 understanding.

 1. Challenge the foundation. Is the overall approach actually right, or is it

 just the first thing that came to mind? Interrogate the architecture, the

 framing, and the core assumptions before touching details. If there's a

 fundamentally better angle — different architecture, different sequencing,

 different tradeoffs, a smaller problem hiding inside the big one — say so and

 restructure around it. Do not anchor to your first answer because you already

 committed to it on the page.

 2. Stress-test every layer as if you're executing it tomorrow. Walk the plan

 end to end and ask: could I actually sit down and build this without hitting a

 wall? Find the hidden assumptions, the vague steps, the dependency tangles, the

 places where things will quietly break at the edges, the spots where "it'll

 just work" is doing real load-bearing work. For every problem: name it, explain

 why it happens, explain what it costs, and give the fix. Diagnosis without

 prescription is useless.

 3. Pressure-test every technical decision against the best available knowledge.

 Architecture, tooling, patterns, data modeling, sequencing — for each one, ask

 whether it's genuinely the strongest option or just the most familiar. Weigh

 it against what actually holds up in production, not what's popular in

 tutorials or trending this quarter. Where real tradeoffs exist between

 competing approaches, surface them honestly and take a clear position on which

 one I should pick and why. No "it depends" cop-outs.

 4. Optimize for the highest-quality result, reached as fast and efficiently as

 possible. Every step should be pulling its weight toward a correct, durable,

 high-quality outcome — not just toward motion. Ruthlessly cut anything that

 adds time without adding quality, and ruthlessly add anything that protects

 quality even if it costs a little time upfront. Sequence the work so that the

 decisions and pieces with the highest leverage on the final result come first,

 so that mistakes get caught as early and cheaply as possible, and so that

 nothing downstream has to be redone because something upstream was rushed.

 Where two paths exist, pick the one that gets to the correct answer with the

 least wasted motion — and say why. Speed comes from not doing the wrong work,

 not from skipping the right work.

 5. Cut the fat, keep the muscle. Depth is welcome. Redundancy, vagueness, and

 ceremony are not. Every element earns its place or it goes. If a step would

 make me stop and ask "what does this actually mean in practice," either make it

 concrete or delete it. If two steps do the same work, collapse them. If a

 section exists because it sounds thorough rather than because it changes the

 outcome, kill it.

 6. Fill the gaps a senior engineer would catch in a design review. What's

 missing that looks minor now and will hurt later? Edge cases, error handling,

 failure modes, prerequisites, migration paths, ordering constraints, and the

 easy-to-punt decisions that get expensive to reverse. Surface them now, while

 they're cheap.

 7. Rewrite the full plan from scratch. Do not patch the original — rebuild it

 with everything above baked in. Make it comprehensive, precise, and immediately

 actionable. Preserve the parts of the original that were already strong —

 rebuilding does not mean discarding what worked. The goal is the best possible

 plan, not the most rewritten one. Then, at the end, list the most important

 changes you made and explain the reasoning behind each one so I can judge

 whether your thinking holds up.

 Take your time. Think deeply. This is the version I'm going to execute from —

 make it the best one you're capable of producing.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shif +tab to cycle)

 What the original plan got right (keep):

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Audit-first framing is correct — the priority-scoring bug alone means every bulk queue action tonight would land at wrong priorities.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - The BLOCKER identification (priority collapse + F9 tier inversion) is real and confirmed against code.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Kill-switch gaps, case-sensitivity bugs, dedup gaps are all real.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Audit-first framing is correct — the priority-scoring bug alone means every bulk queue action tonight would land at wrong priorities.
 The BLOCKER identificati n (priority collapse + F9 tier inversion) i real and confirmed against code.
 Kill-switch gaps, case-se sitivity bugs, dedup gaps are all eal.
 Tu sa bond check is corr ctly scoped a a 5-minute m nual task.
 What the original plan got wrong (rebuild):

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 1. I missed that the priority bug affects 7 strategies, not 5. Session 49's intra_district , cs_funding_recipient , competitor_displacement also route through add_district() → _calculate_priority(strategy, 0, 0,
 0) . That means the 384 pending F1 prospects, all Session 49 auto-queued prospects, AND the new Session 51 seeds all have collapsed priorities. The fix needs a companion reprioritize-pending pass, otherwise
 Steven is still sorting on garbage after the fix.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 2. My F9 "retier to 760-859" recommendation is the wrong fix. The right fix is to remove auto-queue entirely from the pilot and route extracts to Signals tab only. Rationale: auto-queueing from an unvalidated
 extractor fundamentally violates a "60% validation rate before scaling" exit criterion — you can't validate what's already sitting in the queue creating noise. Signals-only with manual promotion naturally
 enforces the criterion and makes the tier debate moot. Strictly better.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 3. "Disable F8 private networks" was a premature shutdown. One agent theorized diocese research will fail; I took that as gospel. Better move: queue ONE network (Archdiocese of Chicago — big, well-known,
 in-territory) and run the research engine against it as a smallest-reversible test. Takes 10 minutes, gives real data, beats debating a theoretical failure mode.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 4. Stage 2 "act on 4 STRONG enriched signals" is dangerous. Those came from Session 44. I don't know when Session 44 was — could be months stale. Bonds expire, leaders leave, budget cycles close. Plan should
 verify freshness before acting, not just act.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 5. The validation-log infrastructure I proposed (JSON file + /validate_compliance command) is over-engineered. If F9 goes Signals-only, Signals tab status transitions (new → surfaced → acted) already track
 validation. No new command needed. Delete.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 6. .lower() alone won't normalize evidence_type correctly. Claude sometimes returns "Board Adoption" or "board-adoption" . Need .lower().replace("-", "_").replace(" ", "_") or a small canonical map. Minor but
 real.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 7. Missing: smoke-test commands are vague. "Local smoke test" isn't actionable. Plan should list the exact Python snippet to run against /tmp/scout_venv .

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 8. Missing: state-validation check on extracted districts. "Springfield" in an IL compliance PDF might get queued under CA if the scanner doesn't cross-check. Need to verify the scanner does per-item state
 matching; if not, it's a hidden BLOCKER.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 9. Missing: rollback plan for priority signature change. add_district(**kwargs) touches every caller. If it breaks, git revert. Should be called out.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 10. Missing: explicit commit discipline. Project rule is one commit per feature for surgical rollback. Plan should name the commits upfront.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 11. Time/cost realism. Total API cost for full plan ~$10. Time is the real constraint — 8 stages is probably two sessions, not one. Plan needs a clean checkpoint if we stop early.

 Session 51 → Session 52 checklist

 Here's what's queued up, grouped by priority:

 🔴 Act on Session 51 build outputs (nothing run yet)

 1. /signal_competitors — rebuild F2 Signals with new BoardDocs-sourced HIGH signals. ⚠️ Old Session 49 noise (7 entries, empty URLs) is still in the Signals tab and will dedup-block. Needs clearing first.

 2. /signal_funding — rebuild F4 Signals with source URLs now captured.

 3. /signal_csta — rebuild with URLs + auto-queue HIGH chapter leaders as csta_partnership .

 4. /prospect_charter_cmos — queue 43 CMOs / 918 schools (then approve in batches).

 5. /prospect_cte_centers — queue 79 centers / 1,009 sending districts.

 6. /prospect_private_networks — queue 24 diocesan/chain networks.

 7. /scan_compliance CA|IL|MA — F9 PILOT , needs ≥60% manual validation before scaling.

 🟡 Tier A spot-check follow-through (URLs now available)

 8. Act on Western Reserve ESC member districts (Willoughby-Eastlake, Painesville, iSTEM) — NOT the ESC itself. The "ESA buys curriculum" framing was wrong; it's PD funding.

 9. Do NOT act on F2 Carlinville/Effingham/U-46/Azusa — confirmed weak/noise.

 🟢 Deferred from prior sessions

 10. Approve 384 F1 intra-district prospects in batches of 5-10 via /prospect_approve .

 11. Tulsa PS bond — vote was April 7 (2 days ago). Check Prop 3 ($104M tech) result → act on Robert F. Burton if it passed.

 12. Verify Google Alert parser with first post-April-9 digest via /signal_scan .

 13. Act on 4 STRONG enriched signals: Tulsa PS, Richardson ISD, Acton-Boxborough, Norwalk PS.

 ---

 - ✅ /tmp/scout_venv still exists (Python 3.14) — safe to reuse for local smoke tests.

 - ⚠️ Old F2 Session 49 signals (7 noise rows, empty URL column) still in Signals tab — will dedup-block the new BoardDocs signals when /signal_competitors runs. Needs clearing before rebuild.

 ---

 My recommendation for order (but you decide):

 1. First, check Tulsa bond result (#11) — time-sensitive, April 7 vote, might need immediate action.

 2. Then clear stale F2 rows so we can rebuild Signals cleanly.

 3. Then decide: F9 compliance pilot (highest-leverage, needs validation) vs. static seed queueing (F6/F7/F8, lowest risk) vs. acting on the 4 STRONG signals.

 things.) I want you to really examine them carefully and how we built them for any issues and suggest changes.

 ⏺ Thinking through this properly before rewriting.

 What the original plan got right (keep):

 - Audit-first framing is correct — the priority-scoring bug alone means every bulk queue action tonight would land at wrong priorities.

 - The BLOCKER identification (priority collapse + F9 tier inversion) is real and confirmed against code.

 - Kill-switch gaps, case-sensitivity bugs, dedup gaps are all real.

 - Tulsa bond check is correctly scoped as a 5-minute manual task.

 What the original plan got wrong (rebuild):

 1. I missed that the priority bug affects 7 strategies, not 5. Session 49's intra_district , cs_funding_recipient , competitor_displacement also route through add_district() → _calculate_priority(strategy, 0, 0,

 0) . That means the 384 pending F1 prospects, all Session 49 auto-queued prospects, AND the new Session 51 seeds all have collapsed priorities. The fix needs a companion reprioritize-pending pass, otherwise

 Steven is still sorting on garbage after the fix.

 2. My F9 "retier to 760-859" recommendation is the wrong fix. The right fix is to remove auto-queue entirely from the pilot and route extracts to Signals tab only. Rationale: auto-queueing from an unvalidated

 extractor fundamentally violates a "60% validation rate before scaling" exit criterion — you can't validate what's already sitting in the queue creating noise. Signals-only with manual promotion naturally

 enforces the criterion and makes the tier debate moot. Strictly better.

 3. "Disable F8 private networks" was a premature shutdown. One agent theorized diocese research will fail; I took that as gospel. Better move: queue ONE network (Archdiocese of Chicago — big, well-known,

 in-territory) and run the research engine against it as a smallest-reversible test. Takes 10 minutes, gives real data, beats debating a theoretical failure mode.

 4. Stage 2 "act on 4 STRONG enriched signals" is dangerous. Those came from Session 44. I don't know when Session 44 was — could be months stale. Bonds expire, leaders leave, budget cycles close. Plan should

 verify freshness before acting, not just act.

 5. The validation-log infrastructure I proposed (JSON file + /validate_compliance command) is over-engineered. If F9 goes Signals-only, Signals tab status transitions (new → surfaced → acted) already track

 validation. No new command needed. Delete.

 6. .lower() alone won't normalize evidence_type correctly. Claude sometimes returns "Board Adoption" or "board-adoption" . Need .lower().replace("-", "_").replace(" ", "_") or a small canonical map. Minor but

 real.

 7. Missing: smoke-test commands are vague. "Local smoke test" isn't actionable. Plan should list the exact Python snippet to run against /tmp/scout_venv .

 8. Missing: state-validation check on extracted districts. "Springfield" in an IL compliance PDF might get queued under CA if the scanner doesn't cross-check. Need to verify the scanner does per-item state

 matching; if not, it's a hidden BLOCKER.

 9. Missing: rollback plan for priority signature change. add_district(**kwargs) touches every caller. If it breaks, git revert. Should be called out.

 10. Missing: explicit commit discipline. Project rule is one commit per feature for surgical rollback. Plan should name the commits upfront.

 11. Time/cost realism. Total API cost for full plan ~$10. Time is the real constraint — 8 stages is probably two sessions, not one. Plan needs a clean checkpoint if we stop early.

 Ready to code?
 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 Session 52 Plan — Audit, Fix, Then Act on Session 51

 Context

 Session 51 shipped F2 rewrite, F5, F6, F7, F8, F9, F10 without plan mode or discussion. Steven asked for an audit before acting. Three Explore agents reviewed the code in parallel and two findings verified

 against live source are serious enough to block bulk queue actions tonight.

 The session is audit → fix → rebuild signals → queue → pilot → backlog , in that order. The sequencing is load-bearing: everything downstream of the priority fix has to wait for it, or we'll be sorting on

 garbage and redoing work.

 A feedback memory ( feedback_always_plan_mode.md ) has been added to prevent autonomous multi-feature builds going forward.

 ---

 The Two Findings That Drive Everything Else

 🔴 BLOCKER A — Priority collapses to tier base for 7 strategies, not just 5

 Where: tools/district_prospector.py:2515 — add_district() calls _calculate_priority(strategy, 0, 0, 0) with positional zeros and no **kwargs . The new branches in _calculate_priority read

 kwargs.get("school_count") , kwargs.get("sending_districts") , kwargs.get("schools") , and est_enrollment — all of which are 0.

 Scope I missed on the first pass: This bug was introduced Session 49 when intra_district , cs_funding_recipient , and competitor_displacement were added. Session 51 extended it to csta_partnership , charter_cmo ,

 cte_center , private_school_network , compliance_gap . Seven strategies total score at tier base with no size scaling.

 Concrete consequence:

 - Every charter CMO queues at 780 regardless of whether it has 3 schools or 135.

 - Every CTE center queues at 760 regardless of sending-district count.

 - The 384 F1 intra-district prospects already in pending are all at 750 flat.

 - The Session 49 auto-queued F2 (competitor_displacement) and F4 (cs_funding_recipient) rows are all at tier base.

 - get_pending(limit=5) returns nonsense sort order.

 Fix requires two things, not one:

 1. Add **kwargs to add_district() signature, forward to _calculate_priority() . Update 5 call sites ( charter_prospector , cte_prospector , private_schools , compliance_gap_scanner , and both scan_competitors +

 scan_csta_chapters in signal_processor ). Populate existing Est. Enrollment , School Count , Total Licenses queue columns from the kwargs (per tools/CLAUDE.md they exist as columns 16-18 but aren't being

 written).

 2. Reprioritize existing pending rows. Otherwise the fix only helps new rows and Steven's queue is still wrong. Add a one-shot reprioritize_pending() function in district_prospector.py that iterates pending

 rows, re-derives strategy + size metadata from notes (or accepts that legacy rows without stored metadata stay at base — document the limitation), and writes the corrected priority back. Call once, then remove

 from session flow.

 Risk of the signature change: add_district() is called from at least 7 places. **kwargs is additive — existing callers that don't pass kwargs continue to work because _calculate_priority treats missing kwargs

 as 0, same as today. No regression risk for upward , cold , winback , proximity , esa_cluster . Rollback = git revert one commit.

 ---

 🔴 BLOCKER B — F9 auto-queue from an unvalidated extractor is structurally broken

 Where: tools/compliance_gap_scanner.py + tools/district_prospector.py:585-592 .

 Two overlapping problems:

 1. Priority tier is 850-939 , docstring says "just below cs_funding_recipient" which is 800-899. 850-939 is numerically above 800-899. Compliance_gap is currently the highest-priority non-upward strategy in the

 entire system.

 2. The scanner auto-queues HIGH-confidence extracts with zero validation data . The session notes say "exit criterion: ≥60% validation rate before scaling." You cannot validate what's already in the queue

 creating noise alongside warm leads.

 the pilot phase :

 - Extracted non_compliant / partial districts at HIGH confidence write to the Signals tab , not the Prospecting Queue.

 - Steven reviews them from /signals like any other signal source.

 - /signal_act N is the existing promotion path — it already queues + triggers research.

 - Validation rate = acted-on vs. extracted. Trackable directly from Signals tab status transitions ( new → acted vs. new → dismissed ). No new log file, no new command.

 - When validation rate ≥60% over 15+ extractions, turn on auto-queue via a kill switch flip and then deal with the tier debate.

 This collapses two fixes into one and deletes a whole sub-plan (the JSON validation log + /validate_compliance command I proposed in the first draft). Delete the tier debate — it doesn't apply until auto-queue

 is on.

 Other Confirmed Issues (SHOULD-FIX Before Running Anything)

 What I'm Deliberately NOT Fixing This Session

 - F8 diocese "research anti-pattern" concern. One reviewer called this a blocker; I disagree. The theory that research_engine will fail on dioceses is testable, not assumed. Test with one network (Archdiocese

 of Chicago — biggest territory diocese, well-documented) and look at the actual output before designing a new research playbook. Queueing 24 networks or disabling all 24 are both premature. One network, ten

 minutes.

 - charter_prospector + cte_prospector consolidation. Near-identical, but consolidation is tech debt, not a bug. Defer.

 - F2 name_to_key fallback silent typos, NCES skip-reason counters, F9 PDF chunk-append overrun edge case, F9 180s timeout deviation from 90s standard, F10 size heuristics. All NITs. Log them as follow-ups in

 SCOUT_PLAN.md at session end; don't fix this session.

 - /validate_compliance + validation log JSON. Obsolete once F9 goes Signals-only.

 Execution Plan

 Stage 0 — Verify the state-qualification assumption (10 min, no cost)

 Before touching any code: read tools/compliance_gap_scanner.py front-to-back (~300 lines) and confirm whether extracted districts are state-validated. If missing, add to Stage 2 as a third BLOCKER fix. If

 present, move on. This is a prerequisite — the plan changes shape if it's missing.

 Also verify the Session 44 "4 STRONG signals" age. Check Signals tab for Tulsa PS / Richardson ISD / Acton-Boxborough / Norwalk PS rows. Note Date + Heat Score . If >30 days old, they're almost certainly stale

 — drop from Stage 1. If fresh, keep.

 Stage 1 — Time-decaying backlog (30 min, ~$0)

 Manual work that can't wait for code fixes and doesn't touch the buggy priority path.

 1. Tulsa PS bond result. Web search: "Tulsa Public Schools" bond "April 7" 2026 results Prop 3 . If Prop 3 ($104M tech bond) passed → surface Robert F. Burton (Exec Dir IT) as a prospect via manual

 /prospect_add + draft outreach via the existing email drafter. If failed → log in SCOUT_HISTORY.md and move on.

 2. 4 STRONG signals — only if Stage 0 confirmed them fresh. For each: manual research + draft outreach. Use prompts/reply_draft.md flow.

 3. Clear the 7 stale Session 49 F2 signals from the Signals tab so the re-scan in Stage 4 doesn't produce duplicate rows. Use signal_processor.update_signal_status(signal_id, "expired") on each — this is the

 existing mechanism, safer than direct row deletion.

 Stage 2 — Fix BLOCKER A (priority collapse) + BLOCKER B (F9 signals-only) (60-90 min)

 One commit per fix per project rule. Sequence:

 Commit 1: fix: add **kwargs forwarding in add_district for priority scaling

 - tools/district_prospector.py

 - add_district(name, state, notes="", strategy="cold", source="manual", signal_id="", **kwargs) — add kwargs

 - Line 2515: priority = _calculate_priority(strategy, 0, 0, kwargs.get("est_enrollment", 0), **kwargs)

 - Populate queue-row columns 16-18 from kwargs: est_enrollment , school_count , total_licenses (the columns already exist per tools/CLAUDE.md ; the prospector just isn't writing them).

 - tools/charter_prospector.py:~queue_charter_cmos — pass school_count=cmo["school_count"], est_enrollment=cmo["est_enrollment"]

 - tools/cte_prospector.py:~queue_cte_centers — pass sending_districts=center["sending_districts"], est_enrollment=center["est_enrollment"]

 - tools/private_schools.py:~queue_private_school_networks — pass schools=network["schools"] (still used even if we don't queue dioceses at scale — the smoke test needs it)

 - tools/signal_processor.py:scan_competitors — pass est_enrollment from NCES lookup result when auto-queueing

 - tools/signal_processor.py:scan_csta_chapters — same

 /tmp/scout_venv/bin/python -c "

 from tools.district_prospector import _calculate_priority

 assert _calculate_priority('charter_cmo', 0, 0, 0, school_count=135) > _calculate_priority('charter_cmo', 0, 0, 0, school_count=3)

 assert _calculate_priority('cte_center', 0, 0, 0, sending_districts=50) > _calculate_priority('cte_center', 0, 0, 0, sending_districts=2)

 assert _calculate_priority('compliance_gap', 0, 0, 50000) > _calculate_priority('compliance_gap', 0, 0, 1000)

 print('priority scaling OK')

 "

 Commit 2: fix: F9 compliance scanner writes to Signals tab, not auto-queue

 - tools/compliance_gap_scanner.py

 - Replace district_prospector.add_district(...) call with signal_processor.write_signals([...]) .

 - Each extracted HIGH non_compliant/partial becomes a signal with signal_type="compliance" , tier=1 , source="compliance_scan" , urgency="time_sensitive" , source_url , evidence_quote in notes/headline.

 promotion via /signal_act; re-enable auto-queue only after 60% validation rate proven over 15+ extractions."

 Commit 5: fix: F9 compliance scanner cost guardrail and parse validation

 - tools/compliance_gap_scanner.py :

 - Module-level _LAST_SCAN: dict[str, datetime] = {}

 - In scan_compliance_gaps(state) : if _LAST_SCAN.get(state) within 24h, return early with a clear message.

 - Wrap Claude JSON parse at lines 221-240 in try/except; on JSONDecode Error or Index Error, log "compliance_scan: claude response parse failed for {pdf_url}, first 200 chars: {raw[:200]}" and return [] . Don't

 mask — the caller's return dict should include parse_errors count.

 Smoke test: run /scan_compliance CA once, then immediately re-run — second should short-circuit with rate-limit message. Revert _LAST_SCAN by module reload or by patching the timestamp down.

 Stage 4 — Rebuild Signals tab with fixed scanners (20 min, ~$1.30)

 Now that priority + case-sensitivity + kill-switch fixes are in, re-run the scanners that got rewritten in Session 51:

 4. /signal_funding — F4 re-run, URLs now captured, ~$0.03.

 5. /signal_competitors — F2 rewrite, BoardDocs queries, ~$0.03. Manual spot-check the first 3 HIGH signals against their source URLs before trusting the auto-queued prospects.

 6. /signal_csta — F5 re-run, ~$1.20. Manual spot-check auto-queued chapter leaders.

 After each: /signals to eyeball the new rows. Confirm Source URL column is populated and Est. Enrollment on any auto-queued prospect is non-zero.

 Stage 5 — F8 single-network smoke test (20 min, ~$0.50 in research costs)

 Queue exactly ONE private-school network: Archdiocese of Chicago (125 schools, IL, in-territory, well-documented, Steven has existing relationships in IL K-12).

 - Manual /prospect_add "Archdiocese of Chicago Schools", IL or run a scoped /prospect_private_networks IL and approve only that row.

 - Let _on_prospect_research_complete run the standard pipeline.

 - Inspect the research output: what did the research_engine find? Contacts? Domains? Is the output useful-enough to drive outreach?

 Decision gate based on output:

 - Useful → unblock /prospect_private_networks full run next session. Log the decision.

 - Half-useful (finds the website but no contact emails) → needs a tweaked research playbook. Draft the playbook requirements in SCOUT_PLAN.md. Do NOT queue the other 23 networks.

 - Useless (research engine errors or produces no actionable data) → flag F8 as needing a fundamental redesign. Document why. Do NOT queue the other 23 networks.

 This is a 10-minute test that either unblocks or surfaces a real problem. Both outcomes are valuable.

 Stage 6 — Queue seed prospectors at correct priorities (20 min, $0)

 7. /list_charter_cmos — read-only view of the 43 CMOs. Confirm seed file is loaded.

 8. /prospect_charter_cmos — queue all 43. Because priority fix is in, top 10 should be the multi-school behemoths (IDEA, KIPP TX, National Heritage, ResponsiveEd, Harmony).

 9. /list_cte_centers — read-only view of the 79 centers.

 10. /prospect_cte_centers — queue all 79. Top 10 should be the OK and OH centers with the most sending districts.

 11. /prospect_all — sanity-check the queue: new seed rows should sort sensibly by priority.

 12. Approve a small first batch (5 from charter, 5 from CTE) via /prospect_approve to start research pipelines. Don't batch-approve everything — let research complete on a small batch first, spot-check

 outputs, THEN approve more.

 Stage 7 — F9 pilot on CA (20 min, $0.50-$2)

 13. /scan_compliance CA — first run. Extractions go to Signals tab (not queue) per BLOCKER B fix.

 14. /signals to review — filter by the new scan.

 15. Manually validate the first 5-10 extracts: does the evidence_quote actually show non-compliance? Does the district match an IL/MA district confused with CA? Cross-reference CDE data for a handful.

 16. Use /signal_act N to promote validated signals to the Prospecting Queue (at the compliance_gap tier, which is still 850-939 — keep for now, revisit once the extractor is proven).

 17. Do NOT run /scan_compliance IL or /scan_compliance MA until ≥3 of the first 5 CA extracts validate. If <3, tune the prompt, re-run CA, try again. That's how the 60% exit criterion actually gets enforced.

 Stage 8 — F1 backlog drip (15 min per batch, $0)

 18. /reprioritize_pending (from Stage 2 Commit 3) has already re-scored the 384 pending F1 rows. Sort is now correct.

 19. /prospect_approve in batches of 5-10. Target: 30 this session (3-6 batches). Rest in future sessions.

 ---

 Rollback Plan

 Every fix is in its own commit. If anything breaks production:

 - git revert <commit-sha> and git push

 - Kill switches on new scanners are the fast path for signal/extractor problems (flip ENABLE_X = False , one-line push, no code logic touched).

 - The add_district(**kwargs) change is additive — existing callers are untouched. Low revert risk.

 - The F9 auto-queue removal is the most impactful change. If we want auto-queue back, it's a single-file revert in compliance_gap_scanner.py .

 ---

 Out of Scope (Logged for Session 53+)

 - charter_prospector + cte_prospector → generic seed_prospector.py consolidation

 - F2 name_to_key fallback logging, NCES skip-reason counters

 - F9 PDF chunk-append overrun edge case

 - F9 180s timeout → 90s project standard alignment

 - F10 homeschool co-op size heuristic improvement

 - Approving the remaining 350+ F1 prospects

 - Scaling F9 beyond CA (gated on ≥60% validation)

 - F8 23 remaining networks (gated on Stage 5 single-network test result)

 - Firecrawl paid plan (budget)

 ---

 What Changed From the First Draft (and Why)

 1. F9 fix changed from "retier to 760-859" to "remove auto-queue, signals-only." Retiering still leaves an unvalidated extractor writing to the queue — fundamentally incompatible with the 60% validation exit

 criterion. Signals-only naturally enforces the criterion via existing /signal_act promotion. Also collapses the tier-debate and deletes the validation-log infrastructure sub-plan entirely. One fix, much

 simpler, strictly better.

 2. Added reprioritize_pending() as a required third commit. Original plan missed that the priority collapse bug wasn't introduced in Session 51 — it was introduced in Session 49 when intra_district ,

 cs_funding_recipient , competitor_displacement were added. 384 F1 pending prospects + all Session 49 auto-queued rows are currently at wrong priority. Fixing only new rows leaves the queue still broken. A

 one-shot reprioritization is the only way to get the whole queue consistent.

 3. F8 approach changed from "disable all 24 networks pending playbook" to "queue 1 network as a smallest-reversible test." One agent theorized research_engine would fail on dioceses. I took that as gospel in

 the first draft and proposed disabling all 24. Better answer: run the actual test with the biggest in-territory diocese (Archdiocese of Chicago). Takes 10 minutes, produces real data, beats debating theory.

 The outcome gates everything else.

 4. Added Stage 0 prerequisite verification. The first draft assumed (a) F9 does per-district state validation and (b) the 4 STRONG Session 44 signals are still fresh. Both are unverified. The plan now reads

 the F9 code + checks signal age before acting. If F9 lacks state validation, it's a third BLOCKER and Stage 2 changes shape. If signals are stale, Stage 1 is shorter. Verifying cheap things cheap is how you

 don't build on sand.

 5. Deleted the /validate_compliance command + JSON log file. Obsolete once F9 goes Signals-only. The Signals tab status transitions already track validation rate implicitly.

 6. Evidence-type normalization got stronger than .lower() . Claude sometimes returns "Board Adoption" or "board-adoption" . Added .replace("-", "_").replace(" ", "_") and encapsulated in a helper so both F2 and

 F5 share the same normalization.

 7. Added concrete smoke-test commands for each fix commit instead of vague "local smoke test." A one-liner Python against /tmp/scout_venv verifies the priority fix in 2 seconds. That's faster than eyeballing a

 diff.

 8. Named commits upfront. Per project rule feedback_per_feature_commits — naming them before starting makes mid-session discipline automatic instead of aspirational, and makes surgical revert possible.

 9. Added clean checkpoints. Session 52 is ~4 hours of work. Steven may not have 4 hours. Explicit checkpoints after Stages 2, 4, and 6 mean we stop at a known-good state instead of mid-fix.

 10. Dropped over-ambitious F1 approval target. Original said "30-50 this session." Revised to "target 30." 50 was aspirational; 30 is realistic given everything else in the session.

 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌

 Claude has written up a plan and is ready to execute. Would you like to proceed?

 ❯ 1. Yes, auto-accept edits

 2. Yes, manually approve edits

 3. No, refine with Ultraplan on Claude Code on the web

 4. Tell Claude what to change

 shift+tab to approve with this feedback

 ctrl-g to edit in VS Code · ~/.claude/plans/dreamy-floating-avalanche.md

 [<u[>1u[>4;2m[<u[>1u[>4;2m
 Yes, auto-accept edits
 ❯ Yes, manually approve edits

 ❯ No, refine with Ultraplan on Claude Code on the web

 ❯ T ell Claude what to change

 '

 Refine this plan. Your job is to pressure-test
 it honestly — keep what's genuinely strong, rebuild what isn't, and hand back
 something I can execute with full confidence.
 Act as a ruthless, senior-level technical reviewer and architect. Be direct,

 specific, and opinionated. No hedging, no diplomacy, no soft language around

 real problems. If something is wrong, say it's wrong and say why. But the

 inverse also holds: if something is right, leave it alone. Do not rewrite solid

 work just to look thorough — churn for its own sake is its own kind of

 failure. The bar for changing something is that the new version is meaningfully

 better, not merely different.

 happen because the reviewer started reacting before they finished

 understanding.

 1. Challenge the foundation. Is the overall approach actually right, or is it

 just the first thing that came to mind? Interrogate the architecture, the

 framing, and the core assumptions before touching details. If there's a

 fundamentally better angle — different architecture, different sequencing,

 different tradeoffs, a smaller problem hiding inside the big one — say so and

 restructure around it. Do not anchor to your first answer because you already

 committed to it on the page.

 2. Stress-test every layer as if you're executing it tomorrow. Walk the plan

 end to end and ask: could I actually sit down and build this without hitting a

 wall? Find the hidden assumptions, the vague steps, the dependency tangles, the

 places where things will quietly break at the edges, the spots where "it'll

 just work" is doing real load-bearing work. For every problem: name it, explain

 why it happens, explain what it costs, and give the fix. Diagnosis without

 prescription is useless.

 3. Pressure-test every technical decision against the best available knowledge.

 Architecture, tooling, patterns, data modeling, sequencing — for each one, ask

 whether it's genuinely the strongest option or just the most familiar. Weigh

 it against what actually holds up in production, not what's popular in

 tutorials or trending this quarter. Where real tradeoffs exist between

 competing approaches, surface them honestly and take a clear position on which

 one I should pick and why. No "it depends" cop-outs.

 4. Optimize for the highest-quality result, reached as fast and efficiently as

 possible. Every step should be pulling its weight toward a correct, durable,

 high-quality outcome — not just toward motion. Ruthlessly cut anything that

 adds time without adding quality, and ruthlessly add anything that protects

 quality even if it costs a little time upfront. Sequence the work so that the

 decisions and pieces with the highest leverage on the final result come first,

 so that mistakes get caught as early and cheaply as possible, and so that

 nothing downstream has to be redone because something upstream was rushed.

 Where two paths exist, pick the one that gets to the correct answer with the

 least wasted motion — and say why. Speed comes from not doing the wrong work,

 not from skipping the right work.

 5. Cut the fat, keep the muscle. Depth is welcome. Redundancy, vagueness, and

 ceremony are not. Every element earns its place or it goes. If a step would

 make me stop and ask "what does this actually mean in practice," either make it

 concrete or delete it. If two steps do the same work, collapse them. If a

 section exists because it sounds thorough rather than because it changes the

 outcome, kill it.

 6. Fill the gaps a senior engineer would catch in a design review. What's

 missing that looks minor now and will hurt later? Edge cases, error handling,

 failure modes, prerequisites, migration paths, ordering constraints, and the

 easy-to-punt decisions that get expensive to reverse. Surface them now, while

 they're cheap.

 7. Rewrite the full plan from scratch. Do not patch the original — rebuild it

 with everything above baked in. Make it comprehensive, precise, and immediately

 actionable. Preserve the parts of the original that were already strong —

 rebuilding does not mean discarding what worked. The goal is the best possible

 plan, not the most rewritten one. Then, at the end, list the most important

 changes you made and explain the reasoning behind each one so I can judge

 whether your thinking holds up.

 Take your time. Think deeply. This is the version I'm going to execute from —

 make it the best one you're capable of producing.

 shift+tab to approve with this feedback

 ctrl-g to edit in VS Code · ~/.claude/plans/dreamy-floating-avalanche.md

 ⏺ Got it. Cached venv at /tmp/scout_venv is still intact (Python 3.14). I've read CLAUDE.md and the top of SCOUT_PLAN.md — Session 51's status is clear.

 Session 51 → Session 52 checklist

 Here's what's queued up, grouped by priority:

 🔴 Act on Session 51 build outputs (nothing run yet)

 1. /signal_competitors — rebuild F2 Signals with new BoardDocs-sourced HIGH signals. ⚠️ Old Session 49 noise (7 entries, empty URLs) is still in the Signals tab and will dedup-block. Needs clearing first.

 2. /signal_funding — rebuild F4 Signals with source URLs now captured.

 3. /signal_csta — rebuild with URLs + auto-queue HIGH chapter leaders as csta_partnership .

 4. /prospect_charter_cmos — queue 43 CMOs / 918 schools (then approve in batches).

 5. /prospect_cte_centers — queue 79 centers / 1,009 sending districts.

 6. /prospect_private_networks — queue 24 diocesan/chain networks.

 7. /scan_compliance CA|IL|MA — F9 PILOT , needs ≥60% manual validation before scaling.

 🟡 Tier A spot-check follow-through (URLs now available)

 8. Act on Western Reserve ESC member districts (Willoughby-Eastlake, Painesville, iSTEM) — NOT the ESC itself. The "ESA buys curriculum" framing was wrong; it's PD funding.

 9. Do NOT act on F2 Carlinville/Effingham/U-46/Azusa — confirmed weak/noise.

 🟢 Deferred from prior sessions

 10. Approve 384 F1 intra-district prospects in batches of 5-10 via /prospect_approve .

 11. Tulsa PS bond — vote was April 7 (2 days ago). Check Prop 3 ($104M tech) result → act on Robert F. Burton if it passed.

 12. Verify Google Alert parser with first post-April-9 digest via /signal_scan .

 13. Act on 4 STRONG enriched signals: Tulsa PS, Richardson ISD, Acton-Boxborough, Norwalk PS.

 ---

 - ✅ /tmp/scout_venv still exists (Python 3.14) — safe to reuse for local smoke tests.

 - ⚠️ Old F2 Session 49 signals (7 noise rows, empty URL column) still in Signals tab — will dedup-block the new BoardDocs signals when /signal_competitors runs. Needs clearing before rebuild.

 ---

 My recommendation for order (but you decide):

 1. First, check Tulsa bond result (#11) — time-sensitive, April 7 vote, might need immediate action.

 2. Then clear stale F2 rows so we can rebuild Signals cleanly.

 3. Then decide: F9 compliance pilot (highest-leverage, needs validation) vs. static seed queueing (F6/F7/F8, lowest risk) vs. acting on the 4 STRONG signals.

 What do you want to tackle first?

 ❯ OK i want to do all of this. Enter plan mode and make a plan. I also want you to look back at these specific things we built last session: "F2 scanner tightening kill false positives

 • F5 CSTA Chapter Partnership

 F10 Homeschoot fo-op Diseovery

 • F6 Charter School CMO Seed List

 • F7 CTE Center Directory

 • F8 Private School via NCES PSS

 • F9 CS Graduation Compliance Gap PDF Pilot" because you built them without entering plan mod eand discussing with me. (Make a note to not do that in the future. Always enter plan mode before building

 things.) I want you to really examine them carefully and how we built them for any issues and suggest changes.

 Claude is now exploring and designing an implementation approach.

 ├─ Examine F2 rewrite and F5 CSTA · 40 tool uses · 69.5k tokens

 │ ⎿ Done

 ├─ Examine F6 F7 F8 F10 prospectors · 35 tool uses · 74.2k tokens

 │ ⎿ Done

 ⏺ Let me verify the two biggest BLOCKER claims before writing the plan.

 ⎿  Loaded tools/CLAUDE.md

 ⏺ Confirmed the two critical findings. Now writing the plan file.

 ⏺ Updated plan

 ⎿  /plan to preview

 ⎿  Tool use rejected

 ⏺ Thinking through this properly before rewriting.

 What the original plan got right (keep):

 - Audit-first framing is correct — the priority-scoring bug alone means every bulk queue action tonight would land at wrong priorities.

 - The BLOCKER identification (priority collapse + F9 tier inversion) is real and confirmed against code.

 - Kill-switch gaps, case-sensitivity bugs, dedup gaps are all real.

 - Tulsa bond check is correctly scoped as a 5-minute manual task.

 What the original plan got wrong (rebuild):

 1. I missed that the priority bug affects 7 strategies, not 5. Session 49's intra_district , cs_funding_recipient , competitor_displacement also route through add_district() → _calculate_priority(strategy, 0, 0,

 0) . That means the 384 pending F1 prospects, all Session 49 auto-queued prospects, AND the new Session 51 seeds all have collapsed priorities. The fix needs a companion reprioritize-pending pass, otherwise

 Steven is still sorting on garbage after the fix.

 2. My F9 "retier to 760-859" recommendation is the wrong fix. The right fix is to remove auto-queue entirely from the pilot and route extracts to Signals tab only. Rationale: auto-queueing from an unvalidated

 extractor fundamentally violates a "60% validation rate before scaling" exit criterion — you can't validate what's already sitting in the queue creating noise. Signals-only with manual promotion naturally

 enforces the criterion and makes the tier debate moot. Strictly better.

 3. "Disable F8 private networks" was a premature shutdown. One agent theorized diocese research will fail; I took that as gospel. Better move: queue ONE network (Archdiocese of Chicago — big, well-known,

 in-territory) and run the research engine against it as a smallest-reversible test. Takes 10 minutes, gives real data, beats debating a theoretical failure mode.

 4. Stage 2 "act on 4 STRONG enriched signals" is dangerous. Those came from Session 44. I don't know when Session 44 was — could be months stale. Bonds expire, leaders leave, budget cycles close. Plan should

 verify freshness before acting, not just act.

 5. The validation-log infrastructure I proposed (JSON file + /validate_compliance command) is over-engineered. If F9 goes Signals-only, Signals tab status transitions (new → surfaced → acted) already track

 validation. No new command needed. Delete.

 6. .lower() alone won't normalize evidence_type correctly. Claude sometimes returns "Board Adoption" or "board-adoption" . Need .lower().replace("-", "_").replace(" ", "_") or a small canonical map. Minor but

 real.

 7. Missing: smoke-test commands are vague. "Local smoke test" isn't actionable. Plan should list the exact Python snippet to run against /tmp/scout_venv .

 8. Missing: state-validation check on extracted districts. "Springfield" in an IL compliance PDF might get queued under CA if the scanner doesn't cross-check. Need to verify the scanner does per-item state

 matching; if not, it's a hidden BLOCKER.

 9. Missing: rollback plan for priority signature change. add_district(**kwargs) touches every caller. If it breaks, git revert. Should be called out.

 10. Missing: explicit commit discipline. Project rule is one commit per feature for surgical rollback. Plan should name the commits upfront.

 11. Time/cost realism. Total API cost for full plan ~$10. Time is the real constraint — 8 stages is probably two sessions, not one. Plan needs a clean checkpoint if we stop early.

 Let me rebuild the plan.

 ✻ thought for 95s)

 ⏺ Walking v2 end-to-end as if I'm executing it tomorrow, I hit twenty real problems. The big ones:

 ⏸ plan mode on (shif +tab to cycle)

 v2's load-bearing weaknesses:

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 1. reprioritize_pending() is underspecified and probably broken as written. For intra_district / cs_funding_recipient / competitor_displacement / csta_partnership rows, there is no stored metadata anywhere —
 they came from scanners that never wrote enrollment back to the queue. The function has no way to recover scores for them unless it does NCES lookups at migration time. v2 hand-waved this as "stay at base for
 legacy." That leaves 384 F1 rows at flat 750, which defeats the purpose of the migration. Real fix: seed-file lookup for charter_cmo / cte_center / private_school_network , NCES enrollment lookup for the
 scanner-based strategies. Meaningfully more work than v2 implied.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 2. /signal_act → compliance_gap strategy mapping is unverified and probably missing. v2's F9 signals-only plan depends on /signal_act N promoting a compliance signal to the compliance_gap strategy. But I never
 checked whether the signal→strategy mapping exists — it's likely hardcoded for bond / leadership / rfp types and has no compliance branch. If I don't add that branch, Stage 7 promotes compliance signals to the
 wrong strategy and everything downstream breaks. Silent hole.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 3. Scanner enrollment-at-queue-time assumes lookup_district_state() returns enrollment. It doesn't. Per tools/CLAUDE.md , the existing helper returns state only. To pass est_enrollment into add_district from
 scan_competitors / scan_csta_chapters , I need a new helper that hits territory_data for the full NCES record. v2 said "pass est_enrollment from NCES lookup" as if the lookup already returns it. It doesn't.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 4. Stage 1 "freshness check" is not a quick prereq. Bonds expire, RFPs close, superintendents leave. "Is this signal still live?" is a 5-10 min manual re-enrichment per signal. 4 signals × 8 min = 30-40 min,
 not a Stage-0 formality. v2 also put the Tulsa bond check in Stage 1 but didn't account for this being comparable manual work.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 5. F8 single-network test success criteria are vague. "Useful / half-useful / useless" is feel, not gate. Needs concrete thresholds: ≥3 extracted contact emails with names+titles, website identified, principal
 or superintendent by name.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 6. Push strategy missing entirely. Every commit triggers Railway redeploy. I need to push after Commits 1-3 (BLOCKERs) as one batch so Stage 4 can use the fixes, then push Commits 4-5 as a second batch. v2
 didn't specify push points at all, which means the scanner re-runs in Stage 4 might execute against stale Railway code.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 7. Stage 5 and 6 should run in parallel, not serial. F8 research-engine runs take 5-15 min in the background. v2 sequences them serially, wasting that window. Stage 6 seed-queueing is independent and can run
 while F8 research completes.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 8. reprioritize_pending — one-off script vs command. v2 flipped on this mid-plan. Pragmatic answer: direct-dispatch command. Runs on Railway with correct creds, triggerable from Telegram, one call and done.
 Accept the tiny ongoing maintenance cost — it's the shortest path.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 9. Honest time estimate. v2 said 4 hours. Walking it step-by-step with realistic commit/test/push cycles, it's 5-8 hours. Likely spans two sessions. The plan needs to say so and define where Session 52 stops
 and Session 53 picks up.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 10. Verify add_district row-write schema before committing the fix. v2 assumed the row-write currently handles 20 columns and I just need to populate 3 of them. I didn't read the actual row-write code. Could
 be 20-column aligned with blanks, could be 13-column leaving 7 unset. Need to read before writing.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 v2's strengths I'm keeping:

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - F9 signals-only approach (vastly better than retiering).

 - F8 one-network smoke-test approach (still the right call, just needs concrete gates).

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Commit discipline + kill-switch pass + evidence-type normalization helper.

 - Stage 0 state-validation prerequisite for F9.

 ⏸ plan mode on (shift+tab to cycle)

 - Clean checkpoint structure.

 Ready to code?
 Here is Claude's plan:
 ╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌╌
 Session 52 Plan — Audit, Fix, Then Act on Session 51

 Context

 Session 51 shipped F2 rewrite, F5, F6, F7, F8, F9, F10 without plan mode. Steven flagged this and asked for a rigorous audit before any bulk acting. Three Explore agents reviewed the code in parallel; two

 findings verified against live source are serious enough to block the entire Session 51 output-acting agenda until fixed, and walking the execution flow end-to-end surfaced several more structural issues below

 the blockers.

 The session is verify prerequisites → fix blockers → rebuild signals → queue seeds → pilot F9 → backlog drip . The sequencing is load-bearing: the priority bug affects seven strategies across Sessions 49 and

 51, and anything downstream of it queued tonight is sorting on garbage.

 feedback_always_plan_mode.md was added to prevent autonomous multi-feature builds going forward.

 This is a 5-8 hour plan. Realistic scope for one session is Stages 0-4 (verify, fix blockers, rebuild scanners) with a clean handoff to Session 53 for Stages 5-8 (F8 test, seed queue, F9 pilot, F1 backlog).

 Checkpoints are marked explicitly.

 ---

 The Two Confirmed Blockers

 🔴 BLOCKER A — Priority scoring collapses for seven strategies

 Where: tools/district_prospector.py:2515 — add_district() calls _calculate_priority(strategy, 0, 0, 0) with positional zeros and no **kwargs . The new branches in _calculate_priority read

 kwargs.get("school_count") , kwargs.get("sending_districts") , kwargs.get("schools") , and est_enrollment — all 0 at the call site.

 Scope: Bug was introduced Session 49 when intra_district / cs_funding_recipient / competitor_displacement were added and extended Session 51 with csta_partnership / charter_cmo / cte_center /

 private_school_network / compliance_gap . Seven strategies total score at tier base with no size scaling.

 Consequences right now:

 - 384 pending F1 intra_district rows at flat 750

 - All Session 49 auto-queued F2/F4 rows at tier base

 - /prospect_charter_cmos would queue every CMO at 780 regardless of whether it has 3 schools or 135

 - /prospect_cte_centers same — every center at 760

 - get_pending() sort order is meaningless for anything Session 49+

 Fix has three parts, not one:

 1. Signature + forwarding — add_district(name, state, notes="", strategy="cold", source="manual", signal_id="", **kwargs) . Forward to _calculate_priority(strategy, 0, 0, kwargs.get("est_enrollment", 0),

 **kwargs) . Prerequisite: read tools/district_prospector.py:2490-2560 first to confirm the row-write schema. I need to know whether the existing write produces 20 aligned columns with blanks or a shorter row,

 because the fix populates Est. Enrollment (col 16), School Count (col 17), Total Licenses (col 18).

 2. Five call-site updates — pass the size kwargs the callers already have:

 - tools/charter_prospector.py : school_count , est_enrollment

 - tools/cte_prospector.py : sending_districts , est_enrollment

 - tools/private_schools.py : schools

 - tools/signal_processor.py:scan_competitors : needs enrollment lookup — see next bullet

 - tools/signal_processor.py:scan_csta_chapters : same

 3. New helper: territory_data.lookup_district_enrollment(name, state) -> int . The existing signal_processor.lookup_district_state() returns state only. To pass enrollment into add_district from the two

 scanners, I need a helper that hits the NCES district dataset and returns enrollment. Add it once, call from both scanners. ~15 lines.

 Then the migration: Fixing future queues doesn't repair existing ones. reprioritize_pending() must recover size metadata for already-queued rows:

 - For charter_cmo / cte_center / private_school_network rows: look up the row's Account Name in the seed JSON files ( memory/charter_cmos.json , memory/cte_centers.json , PRIVATE_SCHOOL_NETWORKS in-memory seed).

 Seeds are authoritative for size metadata.

 - For intra_district / cs_funding_recipient / competitor_displacement / csta_partnership rows: NCES enrollment lookup via the new territory_data.lookup_district_enrollment(name, state) . If the row's district

 name doesn't match NCES, leave at tier base and log the miss.

 - For compliance_gap : shouldn't exist post-fix (F9 becomes signals-only). Any stragglers from Session 51 test runs can be cleared manually.

 Ship as a direct-dispatch Telegram command /reprioritize_pending — runs on Railway with creds, triggerable in one message, one-shot migration + diagnostic tool if we need to re-run later.

 Risk of the signature change: add_district() is called from ~7 places. **kwargs is additive — existing callers continue to work. No regression risk for upward / cold / winback / proximity / esa_cluster .

 Rollback = git revert one commit.

 ---

 🔴 BLOCKER B — F9 auto-queue is fundamentally incompatible with its own exit criterion

 Where: tools/compliance_gap_scanner.py writes HIGH-confidence extracts directly to the Prospecting Queue at priority 850-939 (the highest non-upward tier in the system), while the session notes say "exit

 criterion: ≥60% validation rate before scaling."

 The tier is also numerically contradictory — docstring says "just below cs_funding_recipient (800-899)" but 850-939 is above 800-899. This is secondary though. The real problem is auto-queueing from an

 unvalidated extractor at all. You cannot validate what's already sitting in the queue alongside warm leads because the validation noise contaminates the top of your actionable work.

 Fix: F9 becomes Signals-tab only for the pilot phase.

 - Scanner writes extracts via signal_processor.write_signals([...]) with signal_type="compliance" , tier=1 , source="compliance_scan" , urgency="time_sensitive" , source_url populated, evidence_quote in

 headline/notes.

 - Existing /signal_act N is the promotion path — Steven reviews, validates, promotes manually.

 - Validation rate = acted / extracted over the first 15+ signals, tracked implicitly via Signals tab status transitions. No JSON log file, no new validate command.

 - Leave the compliance_gap tier branch in _calculate_priority alone — it still applies when Steven promotes a signal. The tier debate is deferred until after validation proves the extractor.

 Sub-fix required that v2 missed: The /signal_act handler's signal→strategy mapping must know to set strategy="compliance_gap" when it promotes a signal with signal_type="compliance" . I need to read the

 /signal_act handler path in agent/main.py and verify it either has a compliance branch already or add one. If the mapping is hardcoded for bond/leadership/rfp only, this becomes a sub-fix inside Commit 2.

 ---

 SHOULD-FIX (bundled into Stage 3)

 Explicitly Out of Scope for This Session

 - F8 diocese research-playbook redesign (gated on Stage 5 one-network test)

 - F2 name_to_key fallback logging, NCES skip-reason counters

 - F9 PDF chunk-append overrun edge case, 180s vs 90s timeout alignment

 - F10 homeschool co-op size heuristic improvement

 - charter_prospector + cte_prospector consolidation into seed_prospector.py

 - Approving the full 384 F1 backlog (multi-session work — target 30 this session)

 - Scaling F9 beyond CA (gated on ≥60% validation)

 - Firecrawl paid plan
